---
title: "Team & Player Insights"
subtitle: "Who dominated MPL Cambodia Season 6? A look at team standings and individual star performers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path(".").resolve()))
import utils
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import seaborn as sns

df  = utils.load_data()
ml  = utils.match_level(df)
utils.apply_dark_theme()
```

This page synthesises everything seen so far into a **team-level** and **player-level** ranking. We measure performance across multiple dimensions — wins, gold efficiency, objective control, KDA, and MVP awards — to build a complete picture of who truly dominated MPL Cambodia Season 6.

---

## Team Overview

```{python}
#| label: tbl-team-overview

team_summary = (
    df.groupby("team_clean")
      .agg(
          Games     = ("win_bool", "count"),
          Wins      = ("win_bool", "sum"),
          Avg_KDA   = ("kda",    "mean"),
          Avg_Gold  = ("Gold",   "mean"),
          Avg_K     = ("K",      "mean"),
          Avg_D     = ("D",      "mean"),
          Avg_A     = ("A",      "mean"),
          MVP_total = ("MVP",    "sum"),
      )
      .assign(
          Losses      = lambda x: x["Games"] - x["Wins"],
          # win_bool is True for every player row on the winning side,
          # so mean(win_bool)*100 gives the correct win rate directly.
          Win_Rate    = lambda x: (x["Wins"] / x["Games"] * 100).round(1),
      )
      .reset_index()
)

display = team_summary[[
    "team_clean", "Win_Rate", "Wins", "Losses",
    "Avg_KDA", "Avg_Gold", "Avg_K", "Avg_D", "Avg_A", "MVP_total"
]].sort_values("Win_Rate", ascending=False).round(2)
display.columns = [
    "Team", "Win Rate %", "Wins", "Losses",
    "Avg KDA", "Avg Gold", "Avg K", "Avg D", "Avg A", "Total MVPs"
]

display.style\
    .hide(axis="index")\
    .format({"Avg Gold": "{:,.0f}"})
```

---

## Win Rate by Team

```{python}
#| label: fig-team-winrate
#| fig-cap: "Win rate percentage for each team, ordered from best to worst."

team_wr = display.sort_values("Win Rate %", ascending=True)
colors = [utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(t, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for t in team_wr["Team"]]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
bars = ax.barh(team_wr["Team"], team_wr["Win Rate %"],
               color=colors, edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9, height=0.6)

ax.axvline(50, color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=1.8, ls="--", label="50% baseline")

for bar, val in zip(bars, team_wr["Win Rate %"]):
    ax.text(val + 0.5, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{val:.1f}%", va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=9, color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontweight="bold")

ax.set_xlabel("Win Rate (%)")
ax.set_title("Team Win Rates — MPL Cambodia Season 6", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** A clear tier separation emerges: the top 2–3 teams post win rates well above 50%, while the bottom of the table struggles to take games. This gap reflects both roster quality and strategic preparation — top teams enter drafts with consistent plans while lower-ranked teams show more reactive, inconsistent picks.
:::

---

## Team Gold Efficiency

```{python}
#| label: fig-team-gold
#| fig-cap: "Average gold earned per player per game by team. Higher gold → better farming & fight participation."

team_gold = (
    df.groupby("team_clean")["Gold"]
      .mean()
      .sort_values(ascending=True)
      .reset_index()
)

colors_g = [utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(t, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for t in team_gold["team_clean"]]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
bars = ax.barh(team_gold["team_clean"], team_gold["Gold"],
               color=colors_g, edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9, height=0.6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f"{v/1000:.1f}k"))
for bar, val in zip(bars, team_gold["Gold"]):
    ax.text(val + 50, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{val:,.0f}", va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=8.5, color=utils.PALETTE["text"])
ax.set_xlabel("Avg Gold per Player per Game")
ax.set_title("Average Gold Earned by Team", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, team_gold["Gold"].max() * 1.15)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Team Objective Control

```{python}
#| label: fig-team-objectives
#| fig-cap: "Average objectives secured per game split by type — indicates team macro play strength."

obj_team = (
    ml.groupby("team_clean")[["Tower", "Lord", "Turtle"]]
      .mean()
      .round(2)
      .reset_index()
)
obj_team = obj_team.sort_values("Tower", ascending=False)

x = np.arange(len(obj_team))
w = 0.26
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))

b1 = ax.bar(x - w,   obj_team["Tower"],  w, label="Towers",
            color=utils.PALETTE["accent"],   edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9)
b2 = ax.bar(x,       obj_team["Lord"],   w, label="Lords",
            color=utils.PALETTE["warning"],  edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9)
b3 = ax.bar(x + w,   obj_team["Turtle"], w, label="Turtles",
            color=utils.PALETTE["success"],  edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"], alpha=0.9)

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(obj_team["team_clean"], rotation=20, ha="right", fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel("Avg per Game")
ax.set_title("Team Objective Control — Avg per Game", pad=12)
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Teams at the top of the standings consistently lead in all three objective types. **Tower destruction** is the most reliable indicator of a controlled game — it signals a team that translates kills into map pressure. Lord and Turtle numbers show which teams excel at rotations and timing.
:::

---

## Team Radar — Multi-Metric Comparison

```{python}
#| label: fig-team-radar
#| fig-cap: "Normalised radar chart showing each team's relative strength across 5 dimensions."

from matplotlib.patches import FancyArrowPatch

metrics_radar  = ["Avg KDA", "Avg K", "Avg Gold", "Win Rate %", "Total MVPs"]
display_norm   = display.copy()
for col in metrics_radar:
    col_min = display_norm[col].min()
    col_max = display_norm[col].max()
    display_norm[col] = (display_norm[col] - col_min) / (col_max - col_min + 1e-9)

labels  = ["KDA", "Kills", "Gold", "Win Rate", "MVPs"]
n_vars  = len(labels)
angles  = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, n_vars, endpoint=False).tolist()
angles += angles[:1]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8), subplot_kw=dict(polar=True))
ax.set_facecolor(utils.PALETTE["surface"])
fig.patch.set_facecolor(utils.PALETTE["bg"])

ax.set_theta_offset(np.pi / 2)
ax.set_theta_direction(-1)
ax.set_thetagrids(np.degrees(angles[:-1]), labels,
                  color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.set_yticks([0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0])
ax.set_yticklabels(["25%", "50%", "75%", "100%"],
                   color=utils.PALETTE["muted"], fontsize=7)
ax.grid(color="#e2e8f0", linewidth=0.8)
ax.spines["polar"].set_color("#e2e8f0")

for _, row in display_norm.iterrows():
    team   = row["Team"]
    vals   = [row[m] for m in metrics_radar] + [row[metrics_radar[0]]]
    color  = utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(team, utils.PALETTE["muted"])
    ax.plot(angles, vals, color=color, lw=2, label=team)
    ax.fill(angles, vals, color=color, alpha=0.12)

ax.set_title("Team Comparison — Normalised Radar", pad=25,
             color="#0f172a", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
ax.legend(loc="upper right", bbox_to_anchor=(1.35, 1.15),
          fontsize=8, framealpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** The radar chart reveals that the highest-performing teams score well across **all five dimensions simultaneously** — they are not one-dimensional. Lower-tier teams often spike in one area (e.g. kills) but underperform in win rate or objective control, showing an imbalanced macro strategy.
:::

---

## Top Players by KDA

```{python}
#| label: fig-top-kda
#| fig-cap: "Top 15 players by mean KDA (minimum 10 games played)."

player_stats = (
    df.groupby(["Players", "team_clean"])
      .agg(
          games    = ("K",    "count"),
          avg_kda  = ("kda",  "mean"),
          avg_gold = ("Gold", "mean"),
          avg_k    = ("K",    "mean"),
          avg_d    = ("D",    "mean"),
          avg_a    = ("A",    "mean"),
          mvp_cnt  = ("MVP",  "sum"),
      )
      .reset_index()
)
player_stats = player_stats[player_stats["games"] >= 10].copy()

top_kda = player_stats.sort_values("avg_kda", ascending=False).head(15)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
colors_p = [utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(t, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for t in top_kda["team_clean"]]
bars = ax.barh(
    top_kda["Players"][::-1],
    top_kda["avg_kda"][::-1],
    color=colors_p[::-1],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9, height=0.65,
)
for bar, val in zip(bars, top_kda["avg_kda"][::-1]):
    ax.text(val + 0.05, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{val:.2f}", va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=8.5, color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontweight="bold")

# Legend
handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=t)
    for t, c in utils.TEAM_COLORS.items()
    if t in top_kda["team_clean"].values
]
ax.legend(handles=handles, fontsize=8, framealpha=0.3,
          loc="lower right")
ax.set_xlabel("Average KDA")
ax.set_title("Top 15 Players by Average KDA (min. 10 games)", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, top_kda["avg_kda"].max() * 1.15)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Top Players by Gold

```{python}
#| label: fig-top-gold
#| fig-cap: "Top 15 players by average gold earned per game."

top_gold = player_stats.sort_values("avg_gold", ascending=False).head(15)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
colors_pg = [utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(t, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for t in top_gold["team_clean"]]
bars = ax.barh(
    top_gold["Players"][::-1],
    top_gold["avg_gold"][::-1],
    color=colors_pg[::-1],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9, height=0.65,
)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f"{v/1000:.1f}k"))
for bar, val in zip(bars, top_gold["avg_gold"][::-1]):
    ax.text(val + 50, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{val:,.0f}", va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=8.5, color=utils.PALETTE["text"])
handles_g = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=t)
    for t, c in utils.TEAM_COLORS.items()
    if t in top_gold["team_clean"].values
]
ax.legend(handles=handles_g, fontsize=8, framealpha=0.3, loc="lower right")
ax.set_xlabel("Average Gold per Game")
ax.set_title("Top 15 Players by Average Gold (min. 10 games)", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, top_gold["avg_gold"].max() * 1.15)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** The gold leaderboard is dominated by gold-lane **Marksman** and **Fighter** players who are prioritised in the draft and given the most resources. These players are the primary damage dealers of their teams — their gold efficiency is a direct proxy for their team's farming discipline.
:::

---

## MVP Leaderboard

```{python}
#| label: fig-mvp
#| fig-cap: "Total MVP awards per player — recognising the most consistently impactful performers."

top_mvp = player_stats[player_stats["mvp_cnt"] > 0]\
    .sort_values("mvp_cnt", ascending=False)\
    .head(12)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
colors_mvp = [utils.TEAM_COLORS.get(t, utils.PALETTE["muted"]) for t in top_mvp["team_clean"]]
bars = ax.bar(
    top_mvp["Players"],
    top_mvp["mvp_cnt"],
    color=colors_mvp,
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9, width=0.6,
)
for bar, val in zip(bars, top_mvp["mvp_cnt"]):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, bar.get_height() + 0.1,
            str(int(val)), ha="center", va="bottom",
            fontsize=9, color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontweight="bold")

handles_mvp = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=t)
    for t, c in utils.TEAM_COLORS.items()
    if t in top_mvp["team_clean"].values
]
ax.legend(handles=handles_mvp, fontsize=8, framealpha=0.3)
ax.set_xlabel("Player")
ax.set_ylabel("MVP Awards")
ax.set_title("MVP Award Leaderboard — Top 12 Players", pad=12)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=25)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** MVP awards go to the single most impactful player each game. Players appearing repeatedly on this leaderboard are the consistent **difference-makers** of their teams — usually a star carry or a playmaking support who controls the pace of the game. A concentrated MVP leaderboard also suggests teams that rely on one or two primary threats.
:::

---

## Composite Player Scorecard (Top 20)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-player-scorecard

scorecard = player_stats.copy()

# Normalise each metric 0–1 and compute composite score
for col in ["avg_kda", "avg_gold", "avg_k", "mvp_cnt"]:
    mn, mx = scorecard[col].min(), scorecard[col].max()
    scorecard[f"norm_{col}"] = (scorecard[col] - mn) / (mx - mn + 1e-9)

# Penalise deaths
scorecard["norm_low_d"] = 1 - (scorecard["avg_d"] - scorecard["avg_d"].min()) / \
                              (scorecard["avg_d"].max() - scorecard["avg_d"].min() + 1e-9)

scorecard["composite"] = (
    scorecard["norm_avg_kda"]  * 0.30 +
    scorecard["norm_avg_gold"] * 0.25 +
    scorecard["norm_avg_k"]    * 0.20 +
    scorecard["norm_mvp_cnt"]  * 0.15 +
    scorecard["norm_low_d"]    * 0.10
)

top20 = (
    scorecard
    .sort_values("composite", ascending=False)
    .head(20)
    [["Players", "team_clean", "games", "avg_kda", "avg_gold",
      "avg_k", "avg_d", "avg_a", "mvp_cnt", "composite"]]
    .rename(columns={
        "team_clean": "Team", "games": "Games",
        "avg_kda": "KDA", "avg_gold": "Avg Gold",
        "avg_k": "Avg K", "avg_d": "Avg D", "avg_a": "Avg A",
        "mvp_cnt": "MVPs", "composite": "Score",
    })
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
top20.index += 1  # 1-based ranking

top20.style\
    .format({
        "KDA":      "{:.2f}",
        "Avg Gold": "{:,.0f}",
        "Avg K":    "{:.1f}",
        "Avg D":    "{:.1f}",
        "Avg A":    "{:.1f}",
        "Score":    "{:.3f}",
    })
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Composite Score methodology:** Each player is scored out of 1.0 using a weighted blend of normalised KDA (30%), Gold (25%), Kills (20%), MVP frequency (15%), and inverse Deaths (10%). This is a **descriptive ranking**, not a true skill rating — it favours consistent multi-stat contributors over specialists.
:::

---

## Most Picked Heroes

```{python}
#| label: fig-hero-picks
#| fig-cap: "Top 20 most-picked heroes across the entire tournament."

hero_picks = df["Pick"].value_counts().head(20)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 6))
colors_h = sns.color_palette("tab20", len(hero_picks))[::-1]
bars = ax.barh(
    hero_picks.index[::-1],
    hero_picks.values[::-1],
    color=colors_h,
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9, height=0.65,
)
for bar, val in zip(bars, hero_picks.values[::-1]):
    ax.text(val + 0.3, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            str(val), va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=8.5, color=utils.PALETTE["text"], fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Pick Count")
ax.set_title("Top 20 Most-Picked Heroes — MPL Cambodia S6", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, hero_picks.max() * 1.15)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Hero Win Rate (min. 5 picks)

```{python}
#| label: fig-hero-winrate
#| fig-cap: "Win rate of heroes picked at least 5 times, sorted descending."

hero_wr = (
    df.groupby("Pick")["win_bool"]
      .agg(["sum", "count"])
      .rename(columns={"sum": "Wins", "count": "Picks"})
      .query("Picks >= 5")
      .assign(WinRate=lambda x: x["Wins"] / x["Picks"] * 100)
      .sort_values("WinRate", ascending=False)
      .head(20)
      .reset_index()
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 6))
cmap_vals = hero_wr["WinRate"].values
norm = plt.Normalize(cmap_vals.min(), cmap_vals.max())
colors_wr2 = plt.cm.RdYlGn(norm(cmap_vals))

bars = ax.barh(
    hero_wr["Pick"][::-1],
    hero_wr["WinRate"][::-1],
    color=colors_wr2[::-1],
    edgecolor=utils.PALETTE["bg"],
    alpha=0.9, height=0.65,
)
ax.axvline(50, color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], lw=1.8, ls="--", label="50% baseline")
for bar, val, picks in zip(bars, hero_wr["WinRate"][::-1], hero_wr["Picks"][::-1]):
    ax.text(val + 0.5, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{val:.1f}% ({picks} picks)", va="center", ha="left",
            fontsize=8.5, color=utils.PALETTE["text"])
ax.set_xlabel("Win Rate (%)")
ax.set_title("Top 20 Heroes by Win Rate (min. 5 picks)", pad=12)
ax.set_xlim(0, 120)
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Takeaway:** Heroes with both **high pick rates AND high win rates** are the true meta-defining picks of MPL Cambodia S6 — they were prioritised in draft precisely because teams knew they won more often. Heroes with high win rates but low pick counts may be niche counterpicks that are rarely risked in the highest-stakes games.
:::

---

## Summary

```{python}
#| echo: false
top_team  = display.sort_values("Win Rate %", ascending=False).iloc[0]["Team"]
top_kda_p = top_kda.iloc[0]["Players"]
top_gold_p= top_gold.iloc[0]["Players"]
top_mvp_p = top_mvp.iloc[0]["Players"] if len(top_mvp) else "N/A"
top_hero  = hero_picks.index[0]
top_wr_h  = hero_wr.iloc[0]["Pick"]

print(f"""
🏆 Most successful team   : {top_team}
⚔️  Highest KDA player     : {top_kda_p}
💰 Highest gold earner    : {top_gold_p}
🌟 Most MVP awards        : {top_mvp_p}
🦸 Most picked hero       : {top_hero}
✅ Highest win-rate hero   : {top_wr_h}
""")
```

| Award | Winner |
|---|---|
| 🏆 Most Successful Team | `{python} top_team` |
| ⚔️ Highest KDA | `{python} top_kda_p` |
| 💰 Top Gold Earner | `{python} top_gold_p` |
| 🌟 Most MVPs | `{python} top_mvp_p` |
| 🦸 Most Picked Hero | `{python} top_hero` |
| ✅ Highest Win-Rate Hero | `{python} top_wr_h` |

*All findings are purely descriptive and based on the MPL Cambodia Season 6 match records.*
